sections.title: Header
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **NAICS code** | 561210 |
| **NAICS title** | Facilities Support Services |
| **Source market** | Market 09 — 05_market_discovery.md |
| **VN type** | Process VN (service output = operational readiness outcomes) |
| **Top level** | L7 |
| **Türwächter anchor** | L4 Door Guardian Device → L5 Door & Exit Monitoring → L6e Safety & Compliance Management |
| **Architecture distance** | 2 (from Phase 02b ranking) |

---

sections.title: Core Functional Job (L7)
sections.content:
> Operate and maintain a client's built environment to achieve continuous safety compliance, operational readiness, and regulatory conformance while minimising client-side labour

---

sections.title: L7 — Ecosystem
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Building Operations & Facilities Management Ecosystem |
| **Standard** | Moore 1993 |
| **Description** | Community of FM providers, technology vendors, subcontractors, regulators, and building owners coordinated toward continuous operational readiness of built environments |

---

sections.title: Output Types
sections.content:
Five service engagement types activate different subsets of the process chain:

| Code | Output Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| **IFM** | Integrated FM | Full multi-discipline bundled contract (hard + soft + compliance) |
| **HS** | Hard Services | MEP, fire safety, structural — no soft services |
| **SS** | Soft Services | Cleaning, security, catering, landscaping — no hard services |
| **CFM** | Compliance FM | Regulatory inspection and documentation only |
| **PFM** | Portfolio FM | Multi-site, multi-building management |

---

sections.title: L6 Core Process Steps
sections.content:
Sequential service delivery chain for NAICS 561210.

| Node | Name | Job Family | Output Types |
|---|---|---|---|
| **L6a** | Facility Assessment & Contract Scoping | Assess client site conditions and define service scope and SLA obligations | IFM, HS, SS, CFM, PFM |
| **L6b** | Facility Onboarding & System Setup | Deploy monitoring infrastructure and configure service management platforms at client sites | IFM, HS, SS, PFM |
| **L6c** | Planned Preventive Maintenance | Execute scheduled maintenance tasks to preserve equipment integrity and prevent regulatory non-compliance | IFM, HS, PFM |
| **L6d** | Corrective Maintenance & Repair | Respond to and remediate unplanned failures, incidents, and work orders | IFM, HS, PFM |
| **L6e** | Safety & Regulatory Compliance Management | Inspect, test, document, and certify compliance with fire safety, health, and environmental regulations across client sites | IFM, HS, CFM, PFM |
| **L6f** | Service Reporting & Client Delivery | Report service performance, close work orders, and deliver compliance documentation and SLA evidence to clients | IFM, HS, SS, CFM, PFM |

---

sections.title: L6 Horizontal Processes
sections.content:
Run in parallel across all core steps; do not transform output but are required for safe and legal operation.

| Node | Name | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **(H)** | Workforce & Labour Management | Technician scheduling, certification tracking, and contractor coordination run across every L6 step |
| **(H)** | Supply Chain & Procurement | Spare parts, consumables, and subcontractor sourcing support L6c and L6d |
| **(H)** | IT/OT & Building Automation Systems | BMS, CMMS, and IoT infrastructure underpin all data-dependent steps |
| **(H)** | Quality Assurance & Audit | SLA compliance checks and audit trails apply to all service outputs |
| **(H)** | HSE Management | Health, safety, and environmental compliance governs all on-site operations |

---

sections.title: Output Type Activation Matrix
sections.content:
| | IFM | HS | SS | CFM | PFM |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L6a Assessment & Scoping | x | x | x | x | x |
| L6b Onboarding & Setup | x | x | x | | x |
| L6c Preventive Maintenance | x | x | | | x |
| L6d Corrective Maintenance | x | x | | | x |
| L6e Safety & Compliance | x | x | | x | x |
| L6f Reporting & Delivery | x | x | x | x | x |

**Read a column:** L6e is the only core step activated by Compliance FM — this is the narrow wedge where the Türwächter sits. **Read a row:** L6a and L6f are infrastructure steps activated by all output types — commodity process, low competitive moat.

---

sections.title: L5 Equipment per L6 Node
sections.content:
### L6a — Facility Assessment & Contract Scoping

#### L5: Site Survey & Audit Tools

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Digital laser measurement + floor-plan software (Bluebeam, AutoCAD) | 55% | MEDIUM | Analyst estimates, IFM provider service descriptions |
| Manual tape measure + paper plans | 30% | MEDIUM | Field observation |
| BIM-based digital twin survey (Matterport, NavVis) | 15% | LOW | Emerging adoption; no published split |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Laser Distance Meter
- Floor Plan Editor (software)
- Photo Documentation App

#### L5: Contract & CAFM Onboarding Platform

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Integrated IWMS/CAFM (IBM Tririga, Planon, MRI) | 45% | MEDIUM | Mordor Intelligence FM Software Market 2025 |
| Standalone contract management software | 35% | MEDIUM | |
| Spreadsheet-based (Excel) | 20% | LOW | FM industry observation |

**L4 subsystems:**
- SLA Template Engine
- Asset Register Importer
- Client Portal Provisioning

---

### L6b — Facility Onboarding & System Setup

#### L5: Building Management System (BMS/BAS)

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Wired BAS (BACnet / KNX / Modbus — Siemens, Honeywell, Johnson Controls) | 50% | MEDIUM | MarketsandMarkets FM market report; Mordor Intelligence BMS |
| Cloud-connected smart BMS (IP-based, open API) | 35% | MEDIUM | FM market reports 2025 |
| Legacy proprietary BAS (pre-IP, manufacturer-locked) | 15% | MEDIUM | |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Building Automation Controller (BAC)
- Protocol Gateway (BACnet/KNX/Modbus)
- Energy Metering Unit
- Alarm & Event Processor

#### L5: IoT Sensor Infrastructure

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| LoRaWAN sensors (LPWAN, long range, low power) | 35% | MEDIUM | IoT Analytics, building IoT market estimates |
| BLE/Zigbee mesh sensors (short range, dense deployment) | 35% | MEDIUM | |
| 868 MHz / proprietary RF (EasyWave, EnOcean) | 20% | LOW | No published FM-sector split |
| Cellular IoT (NB-IoT, LTE-M) | 10% | LOW | |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Wireless Sensor Node
- RF Gateway / Concentrator
- Battery Management Unit
- Sensor Firmware Controller

---

### L6c — Planned Preventive Maintenance

#### L5: CMMS (Computerised Maintenance Management System)

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cloud/SaaS CMMS (IBM Maximo, ServiceMax, MaintainX, Fiix) | 55% | MEDIUM | Grand View Research FM Software Market 2025; CMMS segment 32% share per Mordor |
| On-premise CMMS (Infor EAM, Oracle Maintenance Cloud) | 30% | MEDIUM | |
| Spreadsheet / paper-based work orders | 15% | MEDIUM | |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Work Order Engine
- Preventive Maintenance Scheduler
- Asset Register (equipment database)
- Mobile Technician App
- Integration API Layer

#### L5: Mechanical Inspection & Test Equipment

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Handheld digital test instruments (multimeter, thermal camera, vibration analyser) | 60% | LOW | Field observation; no published market split |
| Fixed condition monitoring sensors (permanent attachment) | 30% | LOW | |
| Manual gauges and analogue instruments | 10% | LOW | |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Thermal Imaging Camera
- Vibration Sensor Probe
- Electrical Continuity Tester
- Data Logger

---

### L6d — Corrective Maintenance & Repair

#### L5: Mobile Field Service Platform

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| CMMS mobile app (integrated, same vendor as L6c CMMS) | 55% | LOW | Estimated from CMMS market adoption |
| Standalone field service app (ServiceMax, FieldAware) | 30% | LOW | |
| Telephone / paper-based dispatch | 15% | LOW | |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Work Order Receiver (mobile)
- On-site Parts Lookup Module
- Digital Sign-Off / Closure Form
- GPS Dispatch Tracker

#### L5: Spare Parts & Asset Store

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Centralised FM depot (van stock + warehouse) | 50% | LOW | No published split |
| Just-in-time procurement (direct from supplier) | 35% | LOW | |
| Client-side stockroom (managed by FM provider) | 15% | LOW | |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Barcode / RFID Inventory Scanner
- Parts Reorder Trigger System
- Shelf Storage Racking
- Van Storage Unit

---

### L6e — Safety & Regulatory Compliance Management

*This is the primary insertion point for the Türwächter product.*

#### L5: Door & Exit Monitoring System

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Hardwired electronic alarm systems (door contacts wired to fire panel) | 45% | LOW | No published FM-sector split; inferred from fire safety product market |
| Battery-powered wireless alarm devices (EasyWave, LoRaWAN door sensors) | 35% | LOW | Growing segment per FM IoT adoption data |
| Mechanical-only door alarms (no monitoring, no wireless) | 20% | LOW | Declining; legacy installed base |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Door Guardian Device (mechanical alarm + handle guard) ← **Türwächter anchors here**
- Wireless Radio Transmitter (868 MHz / LoRaWAN / BLE)
- Gateway / BUS Interface (signal aggregator to BMS)
- Battery Power Unit (9V block + level monitor)
- Alarm Horn (piezoelectric, ≥90 dB)

#### L5: Fire Safety Inspection Platform

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Digital CAFM-integrated inspection platform (Planon, Planninq) | 40% | LOW | No published split |
| Standalone mobile inspection app (iAuditor/SafetyCulture, Protex AI) | 35% | LOW | |
| Paper-based inspection logs | 25% | LOW | Declining; compliance pressure reducing paper use |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Inspection Checklist Engine
- Fire Panel Status Interface
- Smoke/Heat Detector Node
- Emergency Lighting Test Unit
- Inspection Report Generator

#### L5: Emergency Lighting Test System

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Automated self-test emergency luminaires (BS EN 62034) | 50% | MEDIUM | Industry standard; widely adopted in EU post-2015 |
| Manual portable test units (handheld testers) | 35% | LOW | |
| Third-party monitoring services | 15% | LOW | |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Self-Test Emergency Luminaire
- Central Battery Controller
- Test Trigger Module
- Failure Reporting Interface

---

### L6f — Service Reporting & Client Delivery

#### L5: FM Analytics & Reporting Platform

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Embedded analytics in IWMS/CAFM | 50% | LOW | CAFM market estimated at $1.26B in 2025 (Future Market Insights) |
| Standalone BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker) | 30% | LOW | |
| Custom client portal / PDF export | 20% | LOW | |

**L4 subsystems:**
- SLA KPI Dashboard
- Work Order Closure Report Engine
- Compliance Log Exporter (PDF/API)
- Client Notification Trigger

#### L5: Compliance Documentation Platform

| Alternative | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Integrated CAFM document module | 45% | LOW | |
| Standalone document management (SharePoint, DocuSign) | 40% | LOW | |
| Paper archive | 15% | LOW | Declining under GDPR and audit requirements |

**L4 subsystems:**
- Inspection Certificate Store
- Audit Trail Logger
- Digital Signature Module
- Regulatory Submission Gateway

---

sections.title: QA Self-Check
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | CFJ is industry-level | PASS | No technology reference — states operational outcome |
| Q2 | No orphan nodes | PASS | All L5/L4 have valid parent references |
| Q3 | Valid level transitions | PASS | L7→L6→L5→L4 transitions followed throughout |
| Q4 | L4 children per L5 ≥ 2 | PASS | All L5 nodes have ≥3 L4 subsystems |
| Q5 | No solution-specific L7/L6 names | PASS | L7 and L6 names are industry process terms |
| Q6 | Naming test (5 spot-checks) | PASS | "CMMS", "BMS", "Door Guardian Device", "Alarm Horn", "Thermal Imaging Camera" — all domain-standard |
| Q7 | Unit count in range | PASS | ~45 units (L7: 1, L6 core: 6, L6 horiz: 5, L5: 14, L4: ~50 entries) |

---

sections.title: Product Fit Summary
sections.content:
| Dimension | Assessment |
|---|---|
| **VN anchor level** | L4 — component within L5 Door & Exit Monitoring System |
| **Entry point** | L6e Safety & Compliance Management — activated by IFM, HS, CFM, PFM (4/5 output types) |
| **Current alternative held** | Mechanical-only door alarms (20% share, declining) — direct displacement opportunity |
| **Adjacent L5 complement** | Fire Safety Inspection Platform — Türwächter event logs feed compliance documentation |
| **BMS integration path** | L5 BMS/BAS (BACnet/KNX) via existing EasyWave→BUS Interface gateway |
| **Key constraint interaction** | C6 (EasyWave lock-in) conflicts with BMS operator preference for BACnet/KNX; gateway required |
| **Buyer in this VN** | FM operator (Facilities Support Services company) — not building owner directly |

---

market_value_network.cfj_statement: Operate and maintain a client's built environment to achieve continuous safety compliance, operational readiness, and regulatory conformance while minimising client-side labour

units.name: Building Operations & Facilities Management Ecosystem
units.description: Community of FM providers, technology vendors, subcontractors, regulators, and building owners coordinated toward continuous operational readiness of built environments

units.name: Facility Assessment & Contract Scoping
units.label: L6a
units.description: Assess client site conditions and define service scope, SLA obligations, and asset baseline

units.name: Facility Onboarding & System Setup
units.label: L6b
units.description: Deploy monitoring infrastructure and configure service management platforms at client sites

units.name: Planned Preventive Maintenance
units.label: L6c
units.description: Execute scheduled maintenance tasks to preserve equipment integrity and prevent regulatory non-compliance

units.name: Corrective Maintenance & Repair
units.label: L6d
units.description: Respond to and remediate unplanned equipment failures, incidents, and work orders

units.name: Safety & Regulatory Compliance Management
units.label: L6e
units.description: Inspect, test, document, and certify compliance with fire safety, health, and environmental regulations across client sites

units.name: Service Reporting & Client Delivery
units.label: L6f
units.description: Report service performance, close work orders, and deliver compliance documentation and SLA evidence to clients

units.name: CMMS
units.description: Computerised Maintenance Management System for work order management, asset tracking, and PPM scheduling
units.source: Grand View Research FM Software Market 2025; Mordor Intelligence

units.name: Building Management System
units.description: Centralised BAS/BMS controlling HVAC, access, fire safety, and energy across client building

alternatives.name: Wired BAS (BACnet/KNX/Modbus)
alternatives.examples: Siemens Desigo, Honeywell EBI, JCI Metasys

units.name: Door & Exit Monitoring System
units.description: Hardware and software platform for detecting, alarming, and logging emergency exit door events for compliance and security

alternatives.name: Hardwired electronic alarm systems
alternatives.examples: Door contacts wired to fire panel

units.source: No published FM-sector split; inferred from fire safety product market data and FM IoT adoption trends

units.name: Door Guardian Device
units.description: Mechanical handle guard with integrated alarm trigger — physical blocking + 95 dB alarm on 45° rotation. Product anchor for Türwächter.

units.name: Wireless Radio Transmitter
units.description: 868 MHz / LoRaWAN / BLE transmitter broadcasting guard-state and alarm events to gateway

units.name: Gateway / BUS Interface
units.description: Aggregates wireless signals from multiple door monitoring devices; relays to BMS or monitoring dashboard

units.name: Battery Power Unit
units.description: 9V replaceable battery block with optional battery-level monitoring and low-battery alert

units.name: Alarm Horn
units.description: Piezoelectric horn emitting ≥90 dB alarm on door guardian activation — mechanical trigger, zero electrical dependency

units.name: Fire Safety Inspection Platform
units.description: Digital platform for scheduling, executing, documenting, and certifying fire safety inspections across client buildings
units.source: No published split; estimated from CAFM adoption and FM digitalisation trend data

units.name: FM Analytics & Reporting Platform
units.description: Analytics and reporting tooling delivering SLA evidence, compliance certificates, and performance dashboards to clients
units.source: Future Market Insights CAFM Market 2025 ($1.26B); no published reporting-split data

product_fit.target_alternative: Battery-powered wireless alarm devices (35% share, growing)
product_fit.key_constraint_interaction: C6 EasyWave protocol lock-in requires BUS gateway for BMS integration; FM operators on BACnet/KNX must procure GfS-specific gateway
